steps.title: Locate the phrase's role
steps.content: The underlined phrase follows the statement that advertisers hesitated to enter television. It gives a specific condition that made advertisers cautious: television set manufacturing had stalled during World War II, limiting the potential audience. That pause helps explain why advertisers doubted television could quickly reach many viewers.

steps.title: Reject unrelated functions
steps.content: Choice A concerns broadcasters' persuasion tactics, which appear later. Choice B mentions programming popularity, which the phrase does not discuss. Choice D brings in radio, but the phrase itself does not compare radio and television. The answer is C.
